declarations.name: PosAttachments
declarations.description: Lists whatever is attached to the current thing

members.name: addToList
members.description:
Adds an attachment visually to the list of attachments.
This only adds an entry to the list, it does not actually upload the attachment.
It is meant to be used after an attachment has been uploaded to update the view.

parameters.name: attachment
parameters.description: The attachment to add to the list.

declarations.name: PosCase
declarations.description:
Defines a template to use if the specified condition is met - to be used with [pos-switch](https://pod-os.org/reference/elements/components/pos-switch/).
See [storybook](https://pod-os.github.io/PodOS/storybook/?path=/story/basics--pos-switch) for an example.

attributes.description: The test only evaluates to true if tests for preceding cases have failed

attributes.description: Test if the resource has the specified property (forward link)

attributes.name: if-rev
attributes.description: Test if the resource is the subject of the specified property (backward link)

attributes.name: if-typeof
attributes.description: Test if the resource is of the specified type

attributes.name: not
attributes.description: Negates the result of the test

declarations.description: Tries fetch an image with the solid authentication, and can visualize http errors like 403 or 404 if this fails. Falls back to classic `` on network errors like CORS. Renders a normal link if even this fails.

declarations.name: PosLabel
declarations.description: Displays a human-readable label of the resource, provided by [Thing.label()](https://pod-os.org/reference/core/classes/thing/#label)

declarations.name: PosList
declarations.description:
Renders a template for each resource in a list. The list is either defined by a relation from the parent resource
or by a class for which instances will be listed.

`pos-list` must contain a `<template>` as a single child component. This template will be used to render each resource
in the list. All PodOS elements within this template will receive the listed resource as their context, e.g. a `pos-label`
will then render the label of each list item.

attributes.name: fetch
attributes.description: Whether listed resources should be fetched before being displayed

attributes.name: if-typeof
attributes.description: URI of a class for which instances will be listed

attributes.name: rel
attributes.description: URI of the predicate to follow

members.name: fetch
members.description: Whether listed resources should be fetched before being displayed

members.name: ifTypeof
members.description: URI of a class for which instances will be listed

members.name: rel
members.description: URI of the predicate to follow

declarations.name: PosPredicate
declarations.description:
Displays an RDF term (predicate) in a human-friendly way, using a provided label. The user can still expand the label
to the full URI to see the actual predicate.

This will only show the predicate itself, not any value. If you want to display a value, use [pos-value](../pos-value) instead.

attributes.name: label
attributes.description: The human-readable label to show for this predicate

attributes.name: uri
attributes.description: The full URI of the predicate

members.name: label
members.description: The human-readable label to show for this predicate

members.name: uri
members.description: The full URI of the predicate

events.name: pod-os:resource-loaded
events.description: Indicates that the resource given in `uri` property has been loaded.

declarations.name: PosRichLink
declarations.description:
Renders a link to a resource. The resource is either identified by the given `uri` property, by the resource in
context (given by a surrounding `pos-resource` element), or by following a relation from or to the resource in
context (`rel` / `rev` attributes).

By default, it renders a label and description of the resource. You can override this by providing custom content as
child elements.

**Important:** This component will fire a `pod-os:link` event when clicked instead of doing a normal browser navigation.
To actually navigate to the link target, you have to listen to the event and then perform the navigation
programmatically. Other link-related behaviours (like open in a new tab and copy link target) are working normally.
This is to support client-side navigation.

attributes.name: rel
attributes.description: Link will be obtained by following the predicate with this URI forward from a resource

attributes.name: rev
attributes.description: Link will be obtained by following the predicate with this URI in reverse from a resource

attributes.name: uri
attributes.description: Link will use this URI

members.name: rel
members.description: Link will be obtained by following the predicate with this URI forward from a resource

members.name: rev
members.description: Link will be obtained by following the predicate with this URI in reverse from a resource

members.name: uri
members.description: Link will use this URI

declarations.name: PosRouter
declarations.description:
The responsibility of pos-router is to handle the `uri` query param, that specifies the URI of the resource that is currently opened.
It reads this query param and informs other components about changes via the `pod-os:route-changed` event.
It also intercepts the URLs from `pod-os:link` events and pushes them as a new `uri` parameter.

attributes.name: mode
attributes.description:
The mode defines what default URI will be used, if no URI param is given

- standalone: reroute to pod-os:dashboard
- pod: reroute to the URI that is shown in the actual browser

members.name: mode
members.description:
The mode defines what default URI will be used, if no URI param is given

- standalone: reroute to pod-os:dashboard
- pod: reroute to the URI that is shown in the actual browser

events.name: pod-os:route-changed
events.description: Emits the new URI that is active

events.description: Fires when a term is entered or selected

declarations.name: PosSwitch
declarations.description:
Selects a child template to render based on properties of the subject resource, usually defined by an ancestor `pos-resource` element.
See [storybook](https://pod-os.github.io/PodOS/storybook/?path=/story/basics--pos-switch) for an example.
See [pos-case](https://pod-os.org/reference/elements/components/pos-switch/pos-case/) for available filter conditions.
